diagnosis.differential_diagnosis: Hyponatremia, Mechanical fall, Hypertension, Atrial fibrillation
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient was admitted for confusion believed to be due to worsening of her dementia, compounded by a recent head injury, bladder infection, and issues with sodium regulation. A head CT showed no bleeding or new abnormalities, and during hospitalization, confusion improved with IV fluids and careful monitoring of sodium levels.
